topic: A person finds out they were adopted through a DNA test
raw_output:
[story 1]

The plastic vial felt impossibly heavy in my palm, the small, sealed evidence of a life I never knew. My hands were trembling so violently I had to set the vial down on the polished wood of the kitchen counter, careful not to let it clatter. The results screen glowed with stark, undeniable lettering: "Match confirmed." A coldness washed over me, starting in my chest and spreading outward until my fingers felt numb. I stared at the screen, my breath catching in a shallow, ragged intake. Who was this person? A complete stranger, a lineage I was suddenly tethered to. I couldn't speak; the air seemed to have evacuated my lungs. I slowly pushed myself away from the counter, stumbling backward until the edge of the cabinet pressed against my spine, trying to anchor myself in a reality that had just fractured.

[story 2]

I reread the report three times, the words blurring into meaningless symbols. My eyes kept darting back to the tiny print, searching for some loophole, some error in the testing. When I finally looked up, the sunlight streaming through the window seemed too bright, too harsh. A strange, hollow sensation settled deep within my core, a profound disorientation. I picked up my phone, my fingers clumsy as they navigated to a search bar, but the words refused to form. My jaw tightened so hard I could feel the ache in my molars. I walked to the window, pressing my forehead against the cool glass, trying to steady the erratic rhythm of my heartbeat. The silence of the room amplified the frantic drumming inside my ears.

[story 3]

The confirmation email sat open on my laptop screen, the blue light illuminating the sudden pallor of my skin. I slowly lowered the device, my gaze fixed on the words detailing the genetic linkage. A deep, unsettling lurch seized my stomach, making me inhale sharply, a sound that felt too loud in the quiet apartment. I stood motionless for a long moment, my feet rooted to the floor as if gravity had suddenly doubled its pull. My hands began to move automatically, gripping the arms of my chair until my knuckles were white. A wave of dizzying disbelief washed over me, leaving me feeling strangely lightheaded, as if the ground beneath me had dissolved. I needed to move, to escape the suffocating clarity of the information.
